definition: Raw facts, figures, or observations recorded in a structured or unstructured form, which can be processed and interpreted to produce information.
The process of selecting a course of action based on information; in a data context, refers specifically to decisions informed or enabled by data and analytics.
The Data Management Body of Knowledge published by DAMA International — the globally recognized framework defining data management knowledge areas, practices, and principles.
A structured evaluation of an organization's capabilities across data management disciplines, resulting in a maturity score that identifies strengths and improvement areas.
A stage on a defined scale (typically 1–5) indicating how systematically and effectively an organization performs a given data management capability.
A defined stakeholder type (e.g., data analyst, business manager, IT lead) used to tailor interview questions to the respondent's role and level of data involvement.
